title: salix coulteri f. parvifolia
intro: Salix coulteri f. parvifolia, also known as Coulter's willow, is a variety of willow plant that belongs to the family Salicaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Salix coulteri f. parvifolia include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing. The plant is often prepared as a salve or infusion in herbal medicine, and can also be used to make a tea, tincture, or ointment.
